page: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g is a General Purpose server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical AMD EPYC cores with multithreading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is network-based with a nominal 1 GB local allocation, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong computational and memory throughput capabilities. Single-core performance via stress-ng and gzip compression tests ranks in the top 10%, while multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests place in the top 25%. Large-block memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks also achieve top-tier status. Database operations and LLM text generation tasks across small, medium, and large models consistently perform in the top 10%, though prompt processing for larger models falls into the middle 50%.

Because the CPU allocation is shared, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for high-density resource requirements, though it may experience resource contention compared to dedicated-core instances. It is highly suitable for memory-intensive general-purpose workloads, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference tasks that benefit from large memory capacities and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities without requiring dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g is an x86_64 General Purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. Lacking a dedicated GPU, it relies on network-based storage with a minimal 1 GB local footprint. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in single-core operations, large-block memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core and web-serving capabilities. While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, the high memory-to-core density makes this instance a cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and CPU-based LLM text generation.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g is a general-purpose server featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and network storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g is a general-purpose server featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier database benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-capacity shared-core server balancing forty-eight vCPUs and large memory capacity for database and multi-threaded workloads.

Top-tier performance in database operations and large-block memory bandwidth
Network-based storage configuration with 1 GB local allocation
